text: Heads up team — we changed the deploy process last sprint. The Confluence deployment runbook is out of date, don't follow it blindly. New flow: staging -> smoke tests -> canary at 5% -> full prod.
wait, the runbook says deploy straight to prod after staging? that's what i did for my first deploy :grimacing:
Yeah that page is from November, we added the canary stage after the January outage. I'll update it... eventually :sweat_smile:
When deploying to prod, always run the smoke tests first. `make smoke` against staging. If any of the 14 checks fail, the deploy stops there. No exceptions.
and the canary bakes for 30 minutes minimum now, not 10. we changed that after ACME-201
got it. is the canary automatic or do I have to promote manually?
Automatic promote if metrics stay clean for 30 min. Manual rollback button is in the deploy dashboard, top right. Anyone on the team can hit it, don't wait for permission.
+1 to that. Rolling back is always cheaper than debugging in prod. Nobody has ever been criticized here for rolling back too eagerly.
should we add this to the runbook? the doc drift is getting bad, i almost followed the old flow last week too
yes, it's on my list. tracking in ACME-247 if someone wants to grab it before me
Reminder since it came up twice this week: If the canary shows >2% error rate, rollback immediately. Don't sit there watching it hoping it recovers.
2% feels tight for services with low traffic tho. checkout-service gets like 50 req/min at night, one flaky request skews the whole number
fair — the threshold is 2% sustained over 5 minutes, not instantaneous. the deploy bot already computes it that way
ah ok that's more reasonable. is latency part of the auto-rollback too?
p99 > 800ms sustained 5 min also triggers it. error rate and latency are the only two auto signals, everything else is human judgement
worth saying: rollback first, investigate after. we lost 40 min on the June 9th incident because someone was debugging a live canary
:point_up: this. war room first, blame later. actually no blame at all lol
